Launch-ready permit intelligence

Need permit clarity fast?
Request the report before the guesswork compounds.

PermitPulse packages permit history, scope signals, and decision-level risk into one report for a specific address. Built for contractors, owners, and diligence teams that need a clean next step across launch jurisdictions.

Request Permit History + Risk Report View redacted sample report

Mobile-first intake. Founder pricing for the public launch. Scope is confirmed after you submit the address.

What the report gives you

Instead of piecing together screenshots and portal notes, you get one read on what the record says, where the risks sit, and what to do next.

✓ Permit timeline and current status pulled into one sequence

✓ Approved scope summary and practical constraints

✓ Risk flags that can change a bid, submission, or close-out call

✓ Routing guidance when the right jurisdiction or portal is not obvious

Founder offer

Three simple tiers for launch.

Start small if you only need routing. Step up when the address affects money, schedule, or diligence. Every tier starts with the same lightweight intake.

Launch pricing applies to covered jurisdictions while capacity lasts.

Founder launch

Portal Check

For teams that need fast routing and a clean read on where to start before they pay for deeper work.

$149 per address
  • Jurisdiction and portal lane confirmation
  • High-signal permit match or mismatch check
  • Recommended next move within one concise reply
Request Portal Check
Most requested

History + Risk Report

The core founder offer for bids, purchases, and address-level diligence when a portal screenshot is not enough.

$349 per address
  • Permit timeline and current status summary
  • Scope framing with meaningful caveats
  • Risk flags and next-step recommendation
Request History + Risk Report
Priority lane

Priority Deep Dive

For the address that already has a deadline, a deal attached, or a visible mismatch that needs more attention.

$499 per address
  • Everything in the core report
  • Priority turnaround target and deeper review notes
  • Escalation guidance when the public record is thin

Covered jurisdictions

Start with the California hub, then drill into a jurisdiction page for the official portal, coverage notes, and a direct path to a Permit History + Risk Report.

Open /california/jurisdictions/

Los Angeles

City permit history, valuation, and scope signals for LADBS addresses.
PORTAL / EPIC-LA

Los Angeles County

Sacramento

Issued permit records, valuation checks, and project triage for city addresses.

Santa Monica

Citizen Access plus recent permit history for remodel and reroof due diligence.

Culver City

Building and MEP permit history for compact city projects and address checks.
LIVE / BEVERLY HILLS

Beverly Hills

Permit review for higher-visibility residential and commercial jobs.

Pasadena

Official portal access and report support for older-home permit history questions.
PORTAL / GLENDALE

Glendale

Status-first permit review and report requests tied to specific addresses.
PORTAL / LONG BEACH

Long Beach

Portal-first research for coastal, multifamily, and commercial permit diligence.
PORTAL / SAN DIEGO

San Diego

City approvals portal access and report support when the address really matters.
FAQ

What launch visitors usually ask first.

Short answers before you submit intake.

Do I need a permit number to request a report?

No. An address is enough to start. Include a permit number or project reference if you already have one.

Which jurisdictions are covered at launch?

Launch pages currently cover Los Angeles, Los Angeles County, Sacramento, Santa Monica, Culver City, Beverly Hills, Pasadena, Glendale, Long Beach, and San Diego.

What happens after I submit intake?

PermitPulse reviews the address, confirms the right founder-offer scope, and follows up with timing plus the recommended next step.

Is this legal advice or a guaranteed records pull?

No. This is public-record research and risk framing. Final authority stays with the jurisdiction, and some documents may require extra authorization or agency processing time.
